entry_requirements.asvab_line_score: No specialty-specific ASVAB line-score minimum was found this session beyond standard AFQT eligibility for general enlistment. Army Bands recruiting/COOL materials describe 42R as gated by a live audition against the Army Bands Audition Standards Manual, not by an ASVAB composite score. UNVERIFIED whether an internal line-score floor also exists beyond general AFQT eligibility.
entry_requirements.education: High school diploma or GED (standard Army Tier 1/Tier 2 rule). No minimum formal music degree is stated as a requirement, but Army Bands describes candidates as expected to already be 'accomplished musicians with performance experience.'
entry_requirements.age_range: 17 (with parental consent) to 42, per standard 2026 Army enlistment policy (branch-profiles.json, this project). No 42R-specific age restriction was found.
entry_requirements.physical: Standard Army accession medical/fitness standards. No instrument-specific physical standard was found.
entry_requirements.other: THE AUDITION IS THE REAL GATE, NOT THE ASVAB. Candidates must pass a live audition (prepared solos/excerpts across multiple styles, judged on sight-reading, musicality, phrasing, tone, intonation, range, and technique) and score at least 2.3 on the Army Bands Audition Standards Manual's technical-proficiency scale; assignment to a large/premier-tier Army band requires at least 2.9 on the field audition. 42R is designated under the Army's Civilian Acquired Skills Program (CASP), which lets qualifying musicians enter at the advanced rank of Specialist (E-4) rather than Private (E-1) because the musical skill is treated as an already-acquired credential.

training_pipeline.location: Fort Jackson (SC), Fort Moore (GA), Fort Sill (OK), or Fort Leonard Wood (MO)
training_pipeline.description: Standard Army BCT, identical to all enlisted accessions (branch-profiles.json, this project).

training_pipeline.location: Armed Forces School of Music, Joint Expeditionary Base Little Creek–Fort Story, Virginia Beach, VA (a tri-service schoolhouse that also trains Navy and Marine Corps musicians)
training_pipeline.description: Ensemble and technical training — sight-reading, style versatility, multi-ensemble performance — before assignment to an Army band.

pay_and_compensation.paygrade_entry: E-4 (Specialist) via the Civilian Acquired Skills Program (CASP) — an advanced-entry rank unusual for a first-term enlisted accession
pay_and_compensation.methodology_note: Computed, not authoritative — (E-4 <2yr basic pay $3,142.00 + BAS $476.95) × 12 = $43,427.40, rounded. Deliberately EXCLUDES BAH because BAH is duty-station-specific and this project's pay-tables.json could not verify a reliable location-specific 2026 figure to primary-source confidence; a location-specific BAH figure would materially raise this total (see data/pay-tables.json, bah.national_number_warning).
pay_and_compensation.source: data/pay-tables.json (this project, retrieved 2026-07-12), derived from DFAS 2026 Military Pay Tables

bonuses.conditions: Music specialties are not typically designated shortage-critical MOSs and historically carry LOW-or-NONE enlistment/reenlistment bonus levels relative to technical, medical, or cyber fields — the CASP advanced-rank entry (E-4) functions as this specialty's primary non-cash incentive instead of a cash bonus. Bonus tables change QUARTERLY; no current-quarter 42R bonus figure was verified this session. Mark UNVERIFIED and check goarmy.com or a recruiter for the live figure before citing any number.
bonuses.source: N/A — no bonus figure sourced this session

career_progression.typical_promotion_timeline: Enters at E-4 via CASP (skipping the standard E-1→E-4 timeline); subsequent promotion follows standard Army NCO timelines — E-5 board eligibility roughly 2-4 years after E-4 entry, with E-6/E-7 section-leader and band-NCOIC roles over a full career.
reenlistment_decision_points: End of initial enlistment contract — reenlist into a specific Army band vs. separate
Competitive audition/lateral move to a premier-tier Army ensemble (e.g., The United States Army Field Band, or 'Pershing's Own' U.S. Army Band at Joint Base Myer-Henderson Hall) for proven performers
advanced_schools_or_certs: Standard Army NCO Professional Military Education (NCOA tracks)
Additional instrument, conducting, or arranging specialty training within Army Bands
career_progression.officer_conversion_path: 42C Band Officer exists as a commissioned path for qualified musicians/conductors per goarmy.com's 42C profile; the specific mechanics (Green-to-Gold vs. direct appointment) were not independently verified in depth this session.

retirement.twenty_year_cliff_note: The pension is all-or-nothing at 20 years of service — there is no partial/prorated pension for separating even one day short of 20 years. Before 20 years, a service member keeps every dollar of their own TSP contributions and earnings (always vested) plus DoD's automatic 1% and matching TSP contributions (vested after 2 years) — but forfeits the entire defined-benefit pension.
retirement.continuation_pay_note: A one-time cash payout available between roughly year 7-12 of service (exact window and multiplier set annually per service; Army's published active-component rate is 2.5x monthly basic pay) in exchange for an additional service obligation (statutory minimum 3 years; Army requires 4).
retirement.guard_reserve_points_note: LABELED, NOT INTERCHANGEABLE WITH ACTIVE-DUTY RETIREMENT: Guard/Reserve retirement uses a points system (1 point/day active duty, 1 point/drill, up to 15 membership points/year) toward a 20-qualifying-year non-regular retirement that generally begins paying at age 60 (reducible, but not below age 50, via qualifying active-duty credit).
retirement.lump_sum_option_note: At retirement (20+ years), a member may elect a 25% or 50% lump-sum payout of the discounted present value of future retired pay, in exchange for reduced monthly pay until Full Social Security Retirement Age.
retirement.source: data/retirement.json (this project, retrieved 2026-07-12), derived from myarmybenefits.us.army.mil and myairforcebenefits.us.af.mil

transition.skillbridge_notes: Up to 180 days of permissive duty in a service member's final 180 days on active duty, working with an approved civilian industry partner while still drawing full military pay/benefits. The real gate is unit-commander approval — discretionary, not an entitlement, and can be denied based on unit mission needs.
transition.tap_program_summary: Mandatory DoD-wide Transition Assistance Program: individualized counseling, a VA Benefits course, a DOL employment-fundamentals workshop, an optional 2-day track (higher education / career-technical / entrepreneurship), and a Capstone confirming Career Readiness Standards are met. Must begin no later than 365 days before separation.
transition.dd214_significance: The DD-214 governs eligibility for nearly every downstream veteran benefit — VA disability, VA healthcare, GI Bill, VA home loan, and most state benefits — and Block 24 ('character of service') gates eligibility before any other criterion is considered.
va_disability_common_claims: Tinnitus and bilateral hearing loss — this is a REASONED INFERENCE, not sourced to a musician-specific VA claims dataset this session: sustained exposure to brass/percussion ensemble sound levels and outdoor marching/ceremonial performance plausibly extends VA's general noise-induced-hearing-loss pattern (VA's single most-compensated condition nationwide per data/transition.json) to this specialty. Flagged as inference, not a confirmed statistic — verify against VA claims data by MOS if this figure matters for a specific decision.
transition.vr_e_eligibility_note: Requires a service-connected disability rating of 20%+ creating an 'employment handicap,' generally filed within a 12-year window from separation or first VA rating; funds an approved training/education plan plus a monthly subsistence allowance, and draws from a separate entitlement bucket than the Post-9/11 GI Bill.
transition.source: data/transition.json (this project, retrieved 2026-07-12), derived from militaryonesource.mil, tapevents.mil, va.gov, and archives.gov

example_civilian_titles: K-12 or university music educator (with additional teaching credential)
relevant_certifications: No civilian performance license is required to work as a musician
State K-12 teaching certification (separate process, if pursuing music education)
University-level teaching posts typically require a terminal degree (DMA/MM) that military service does not confer
civilian_crosswalk.credential_gap_note: Military band service functions AS a credential in this field — professional ensembles hire by live/recorded audition, not academic degree, so an Army Band service record and audition history carry real weight. But military service does NOT confer a music degree, a state K-12 teaching certificate, or the terminal degree most university teaching posts require. The civilian working-musician market is also largely gig-based/part-time; BLS's hourly median understates how inconsistent income is for most non-elite performers — see methodology note on the salary range below.
civilian_crosswalk.source: BLS OEWS, Musicians and Singers (27-2042.00): median $42.45/hr, 10th percentile $18.68/hr, 90th percentile $105.44/hr, May 2024 data. Range above is a full-time-equivalent annualization (hourly × 2080) — COMPUTED, NOT AUTHORITATIVE — chosen because BLS reports this occupation hourly, not as an annual salary; most working musicians do not work 2080 hours/year at this rate, so treat the low end as more representative of typical experience and the high end as an outlier for elite/highly-employed performers.

veteran_benefits.gi_bill_tier_note: Post-9/11 GI Bill benefit tier scales with cumulative qualifying active-duty days, from 50% (90-179 days) up to 100% (36+ months, or Purple Heart, or service-connected-disability discharge after 30+ days). No tier below 50%.
veteran_benefits.gi_bill_transferability_note: Transferable to spouse and/or dependent children (up to 36 months total) only while still serving, after 6+ years of service, in exchange for a 4-year additional service obligation — a real service commitment, not paperwork-only. Dependent children must wait until the member reaches 10 years of service to start using it.
veteran_benefits.va_home_loan_note: Basic entitlement of $36,000 guarantees loans up to $144,000; veterans with full entitlement (no unrestored prior VA loan) face no VA-imposed statutory loan limit, though the lender still underwrites on credit/income/DTI. No down payment or monthly PMI is typically required; a funding fee applies unless the veteran is exempt (e.g., receiving VA disability compensation).
veteran_benefits.va_healthcare_priority_group_note: VA assigns one of 8 priority groups at enrollment based on a combination of disability rating, combat/toxic-exposure service history, income relative to VA's geographically-adjusted limits, and other VA benefit receipt — not a single number. Groups 1-3 generally carry no copays; Groups 7-8 owe copays based on income.
veteran_benefits.state_benefit_variance_note: NOT a single national number. State-administered benefits (property tax exemptions, income tax exclusions, tuition waivers) vary enormously by state and even county — e.g., a 100%-disabled veteran gets a full homestead exemption in Texas but a flat $100,000 assessed-value exemption in California. Always verify against the veteran's state of legal residence.
veteran_benefits.source: data/veteran-benefits.json (this project, retrieved 2026-07-12), derived from va.gov, comptroller.texas.gov, and boe.ca.gov

education_benefits.gi_bill_summary: Post-9/11 GI Bill (Ch. 33) pays full net tuition/fees at public schools (in-state rate regardless of residency) with no dollar cap, or up to $29,920.95/year at private/foreign schools (2025-2026 AY), plus a housing allowance and up to $1,000/year book stipend, prorated by eligibility tier.
education_benefits.tuition_assistance: Army Tuition Assistance: $4,500/fiscal-year cap at $250/semester-hour, standard DoD-wide rate. Search-sourced (not independently fetched this session) detail suggests a 16-semester-hour/FY usage limit and 2025-2026 rule tightening — confirm at myarmybenefits.us.army.mil before publishing.
education_benefits.credentialing_assistance: Army Credentialing Assistance (COOL): $2,000/fiscal-year cap (reduced from $4,000 in 2024), lifetime limit reported at 3 credentials per 10 years of service; officers reported ineligible. Search-sourced, not independently fetched from cool.osd.mil this session.

deployment_and_lifestyle.typical_tempo: Garrison-based. Army Bands perform frequent ceremonial and community-relations duties (change-of-command, military-honors funerals, parades, concerts) rather than deploying to combat. Some Army bands do deploy in a ceremonial/morale role in support of overseas contingency operations, but overall tempo and predictability are markedly different from a deployable combat-arms or logistics MOS.
deployment_and_lifestyle.family_considerations: The relatively stable, garrison-centered assignment pattern is often cited as family-friendly compared to high-OPTEMPO deployable units, though evening/weekend ceremonial performance commitments are common and vary significantly by whether the assignment is an installation band, a premier Washington-DC-area ensemble, or the touring Army Field Band.

specialties.name: Musician — U.S. Marine Band "The President's Own" (premier ensemble)
specialties.code: 5500 (Basic Musician) accession lineage, filled via the U.S. Marine Band's own independent audition program rather than the general Marine Corps Musician Enlistment Option Program (MEOP). The exact internal designator specific to the premier-band track was not independently confirmed beyond the general 5500 Basic Musician MOS this session.

entry_requirements.asvab_line_score: Applicants must pass the ASVAB and a full MEPS physical, but the ASVAB is a general-eligibility gate only. Placement is decided entirely by a live performance audition adjudicated by sitting Marine Band musicians — no ASVAB line-score minimum governs selection.
entry_requirements.education: No formal minimum education requirement was found beyond standard enlistment eligibility. In practice, competitive candidates typically hold or are pursuing a conservatory/university-level music degree, consistent with Marine Band career materials describing professional-orchestra-caliber expected musicianship — this is a market reality, not a published formal requirement.
entry_requirements.age_range: 17 to 28 at enlistment per current Marine Band recruiting materials; age waivers may be granted up to 34 for applicants with no prior military service.
entry_requirements.physical: Standard Marine Corps height/weight and medical-examination standards, both at enlistment and throughout the Marine Corps career.
entry_requirements.security_clearance: Secret clearance with Category III White House access — required because of the ensemble's White House and state-function performance mission.
entry_requirements.other: THE AUDITION IS THE ENTIRE GATE, NOT THE ASVAB, AND NOT EVEN BOOT CAMP. Selection is by live audition against a published instrument-specific excerpt/solo list, judged by sitting Marine Band musicians. By federal statute, members of 'The President's Own' are EXEMPT from Marine Corps recruit training entirely — one of only two U.S. military units (the other is the U.S. Coast Guard Band) whose members never attend boot camp. Selected musicians enlist directly at the advanced paygrade of Staff Sergeant (E-6) under a 4-year contract 'for duty with the U.S. Marine Band only,' and remain subject to the UCMJ and standard physical/conduct standards despite skipping recruit training. This is the single most extreme illustration in this cluster of 'the audition, not the ASVAB, is the real barrier.'

training_pipeline.location: Marine Barracks Washington, D.C. (in-person and recorded/regional auditions depending on the current vacancy)
training_pipeline.description: The audition IS the qualifying event. There is no boot camp and no follow-on formal military school stage for this premier ensemble — selected musicians report directly to the Marine Band under their 4-year, band-only contract.

veteran_benefits.state_benefit_variance_note: NOT a single national number — verify against the veteran's state of legal residence.

education_benefits.gi_bill_summary: Post-9/11 GI Bill (Ch. 33) pays full net tuition/fees at public schools with no dollar cap, or up to $29,920.95/year at private/foreign schools (2025-2026 AY), plus housing allowance and book stipend.
education_benefits.tuition_assistance: Navy Tuition Assistance: $4,500/fiscal-year cap at $250/semester-hour, administered through the Navy College Program; an 18-semester-hour/FY usage limit is reported but UNVERIFIED at primary-source level.
education_benefits.credentialing_assistance: Navy Credentialing Opportunities On-Line (COOL) funds credential exams/renewals for active-duty and Reserve Navy (excluding IRR/VTU); specific dollar cap UNVERIFIED — direct fetch to cool.osd.mil failed this project's EDU-BENEFITS pass.

deployment_and_lifestyle.typical_tempo: Navy Musicians serve in fleet-support and shore-based Navy bands worldwide; duty is predominantly ceremonial and community-relations performance rather than open-ocean underway sea duty, though specific unaccompanied/OCONUS band tours were not independently detailed this session.
deployment_and_lifestyle.family_considerations: Generally more stable and garrison-like than a typical sea-duty rating, though specific band assignments (fleet band vs. premier DC-area Navy Band) significantly change the travel/tempo picture.

entry_requirements.asvab_line_score: UNVERIFIED — a specific minimum on the Air Force's General (G) aptitude composite was not found for 3N1X1 this session.
entry_requirements.education: High school diploma or GED equivalency is mandatory. Courses in applied instrumental/vocal music, composition, music theory, music history, harmony, ear training, and/or transposition are 'desirable' per official entry/retention standards, not mandatory.
entry_requirements.age_range: Standard Air Force enlistment age range (17, with parental consent, to 42 per secondary reporting in branch-profiles.json) — not specialty-specific and not independently confirmed on airforce.com this session.
entry_requirements.physical: Standard Air Force accession medical standards; no instrument-specific physical standard was found.
entry_requirements.other: Entry requires passing a mandatory standardized audition evaluated by a band commander — instrumentalists on solo/ensemble performance technique and sight-reading, vocalists additionally on foreign-language singing ability, arrangers on transposition and arranging-software proficiency — as defined in the 3N1 Career Field Education and Training Plan (CFETP). The audition is the substantive qualifying gate, not the ASVAB.

training_pipeline.location: Joint Base San Antonio-Lackland, San Antonio, TX
training_pipeline.description: Standard Air Force BMT (commonly described as ~7.5 weeks; recorded as 7 because this schema field is an integer). Musicians may complete BMT as part of the Drum and Bugle Corps flight (branch-profiles.json, this project).
Award of the 3-skill level is fulfilled by passing the standardized band audition rather than a separate formal technical school; on-the-job qualification at the gaining band follows.

pay_and_compensation.paygrade_entry: E-1 (standard accession). No advanced-rank program comparable to the Army's CASP or the Marine Band's direct-E-6 accession was found for 3N1X1 this session — this is a gap in this session's research, not a confirmed absence of such a program.
pay_and_compensation.methodology_note: Computed, not authoritative — (E-1 <2yr basic pay $2,407.20 + BAS $476.95) × 12 = $34,609.80, rounded. Excludes BAH.

reenlistment_decision_points: Competitive audition/lateral move to a premier ensemble — The USAF Band (Joint Base Anacostia-Bolling, Washington, D.C.) or the USAF Academy Band — for proven performers

career_progression.officer_conversion_path: UNVERIFIED — a musician-specific officer conversion path was not independently confirmed this session.

retirement.continuation_pay_note: A one-time cash payout available roughly between year 7-12 of service (exact window/multiplier set annually per service) in exchange for an additional service obligation (statutory minimum 3 years). specialties.code: CODE STATUS: UNVERIFIED-CURRENT. Historically MOS 4341 Combat Correspondent — still referenced as the MOS awarded by DINFOS's Combat Correspondent Course per DINFOS's own training-department page (secondary-corroborated 2026-07-12). Administratively, the Marine Corps merged the legacy 43XX (Public Affairs) and 46XX (Combat Camera) fields into a new 45XX (Communication Strategy and Operations) occupational field per MARADMIN 484/17 (30 Aug 2017) and MARADMIN 534/17 (28 Sep 2017). entry_requirements.asvab_line_score: Not the real gate — see 'other' below. Standard Coast Guard enlistment eligibility (AFQT ≥ 32 with a diploma, per data/branch-profiles.json) applies as a baseline, but placement is decided entirely by a live audition, consistent with every other musician record in this file.
entry_requirements.education: No formal minimum education requirement beyond standard Coast Guard enlistment eligibility was located this session. In practice, competitive candidates for a rating this small and audition-gated typically hold or are pursuing a conservatory/university-level music degree — a market reality inferred by direct analogy to the U.S. Marine Band and Navy/Army/Air Force band records in this file, NOT independently confirmed for the Coast Guard Band specifically this session.
entry_requirements.age_range: UNVERIFIED — secondary sources (search synthesis) report a general Coast Guard Band applicant ceiling of 'not having reached one's 42nd birthday,' consistent with the Coast Guard's general enlisted age ceiling elsewhere in this project (data/branch-profiles.json), but this was not independently confirmed against a primary uscg.mil source this session (uscg.mil blocked direct fetch with HTTP 403 throughout this session, including with the trailing-slash/full-browser-header technique used successfully elsewhere in this project for gocoastguard.com).
entry_requirements.physical: Standard Coast Guard accession medical standards; no instrument-specific physical standard was found.
entry_requirements.other: THE AUDITION IS THE ENTIRE GATE, NOT THE ASVAB, AND NOT EVEN BOOT CAMP. The Coast Guard Band, based at the U.S. Coast Guard Academy in New London, CT, recruits musicians through an extremely competitive live-audition process (required repertoire, orchestral excerpts, and sight-reading before a panel) rather than the standard enlisted accession pipeline. Per multiple independently-operated secondary sources converging on the same claim (search-corroborated this session; NOT independently confirmed against a primary uscg.mil source because uscg.mil blocked this session's fetch attempts): selected musicians enlist directly at the advanced paygrade of Musician First Class (E-6) under a four-year contract, and — like the U.S. Marine Band 'The President's Own' documented elsewhere in this file — do NOT attend the standard 8-week Coast Guard boot camp at Training Center Cape May, instead completing a much shorter indoctrination period. This session could not independently verify the exact indoctrination length (one secondary source states '2 weeks,' UNVERIFIED). The Marine Band record in this file, sourced to Wikipedia and a secondary feature article, independently states that Marine Band and Coast Guard Band members are 'the only members of the United States Armed Forces not required to undergo recruit training' — the Coast Guard Band's boot-camp exemption is corroborated by that same cross-reference, not solely by this record's own sourcing.

training_pipeline.location: U.S. Coast Guard Academy, New London, CT (live and, per uscg.mil audition-packet filenames found via search, virtual 'mock audition' options)
training_pipeline.description: The audition IS the qualifying event — prepared repertoire, orchestral excerpts, and sight-reading, judged by sitting Coast Guard Band musicians against instrument-specific audition packets (e.g., '2025 Oboe Audition Packet,' '2025 Trumpet Audition Packet' — filenames found via search on uscg.mil, not independently fetched this session because uscg.mil blocked direct access).

training_pipeline.stage: Indoctrination (in lieu of standard recruit training)
training_pipeline.location: Reported as Training Center Cape May, NJ by secondary sources (consistent with the Coast Guard's sole enlisted accession point, data/branch-profiles.json), UNVERIFIED for the Coast Guard Band specifically
training_pipeline.description: Secondary-source-reported (UNVERIFIED at primary level) short indoctrination in place of the standard 8-week Coast Guard boot camp, consistent with the Marine Band's documented recruit-training exemption elsewhere in this file. Selected musicians report to the Coast Guard Band under their enlistment contract following this indoctrination.

retirement.twenty_year_cliff_note: The pension is all-or-nothing at 20 years of service — there is no partial/prorated pension for separating even one day short of 20 years. Before 20 years, a service member keeps every dollar of their own TSP contributions and earnings (always vested), plus DoD's automatic 1%-of-pay TSP contribution and its match (vested after 2 years of service) — all portable into a civilian 401(k)/IRA. The defined-benefit pension itself is 'entirely forfeited' below 20 years, per DoD's own language. This applies identically regardless of specialty or branch, including the Coast Guard (DHS, not DoD, but administered on the same BRS structure).
retirement.continuation_pay_note: One-time cash payout available between roughly year 7 and year 12 of service (exact window and multiplier set annually per branch; Army's published active-component rate, used elsewhere in this project as the reference figure, is 2.5x monthly basic pay for a 4-year additional service obligation). No specialty-specific continuation-pay rate was identified this session for this record — treat the general BRS figure as the baseline and verify the current branch-specific amount before relying on it.
retirement.guard_reserve_points_note: Guard/Reserve retirement is NOT interchangeable with active-duty retirement. It runs on a points system (1 point/drill, 1 point/AD day, up to 15 membership points/year) toward a 20-'good year' qualifying threshold, with a non-regular pension that generally starts at age 60 (reducible, not below age 50, for qualifying post-2008 mobilized duty). Applies identically to this specialty as to any other.
retirement.lump_sum_option_note: At 20+ years, a retiring member may elect 25% or 50% of the discounted present value of future retired pay as an immediate lump sum, in exchange for reduced monthly retired pay until full Social Security retirement age (~67), when the monthly amount returns to the uncut level. Fully taxable in the year received.

transition.skillbridge_notes: UNVERIFIED, consistent with the Marine Band record in this file: standard DoD/DHS SkillBridge policy (permissive duty in the final window before separation, subject to unit-commander approval) was not independently confirmed as applied to this small, audition-only rating's unique band-only enlistment structure this session.
transition.tap_program_summary: Mandatory DoD-wide Transition Assistance Program: pre-separation counseling, a VA Benefits I course, a DOL employment-fundamentals workshop, and an optional 2-day track (Higher Education, Career/Technical Training, or Entrepreneurship/Boots to Business), ending in a Capstone that certifies Career Readiness Standards are met. Must begin no later than 365 days before separation. Applies identically regardless of specialty.
transition.dd214_significance: The DD-214's 'character of service' entry gates eligibility for nearly every benefit below — VA disability, VA healthcare, GI Bill, VA home loan, and most state veteran benefits. No specialty-specific significance beyond the general rule.
va_disability_common_claims: Tinnitus and bilateral hearing loss — REASONED INFERENCE, not sourced to a musician-specific VA claims dataset this session, extending the same noise-exposure reasoning already applied to every other musician record in this file (Army 42R, Marine Band, Navy MU, Air Force 3N1X1) to sustained ensemble/ceremonial-performance sound-level exposure.
transition.vr_e_eligibility_note: Requires a 20%+ service-connected disability rating creating an employment handicap, filed within a 12-year window from separation or first rating. Funds up to 48 months of training/education plus a monthly subsistence allowance (FY2026 max ~$3,439.23/mo per data/transition.json), drawn from a separate entitlement bucket than the Post-9/11 GI Bill. No specialty-specific eligibility variance.

example_civilian_titles: University-level applied music faculty (with terminal degree)
relevant_certifications: No civilian performance license is required to work as a musician
University teaching posts typically require a terminal degree (DMA/MM) that military service does not confer
civilian_crosswalk.credential_gap_note: Coast Guard Band service functions AS a credential in this field, the same way Army/Navy/Air Force/Marine Band service does elsewhere in this file — professional ensembles hire by live/recorded audition, not academic degree, so a Coast Guard Band service record and audition history carry real weight. But military service does NOT confer a music degree, a state K-12 teaching certificate, or the terminal degree most university teaching posts require. The civilian working-musician market is also largely gig-based/part-time; BLS's hourly median understates how inconsistent income is for most non-elite performers.
civilian_crosswalk.source: BLS OEWS, Musicians and Singers (27-2042.00): median $42.45/hr, 10th percentile $18.68/hr, 90th percentile $105.44/hr, May 2024 data — figures reused directly from this file's other musician records for the same occupation code. Range is a full-time-equivalent annualization (hourly × 2080) — COMPUTED, NOT AUTHORITATIVE.

veteran_benefits.gi_bill_tier_note: Post-9/11 GI Bill (Ch. 33) benefit tier scales with cumulative qualifying active-duty days: 100% at 36+ months (or Purple Heart / service-connected-disability discharge after 30+ days), down through 90%, 80%, 70%, 60%, to a 50% floor at 90 days-5 months. No tier below 50%. Only qualifying Title 10 (or, for the Coast Guard, Title 14) active-duty days count. Applies identically regardless of specialty.
veteran_benefits.gi_bill_transferability_note: Transferable to a spouse and/or dependent children (up to 36 months combined) after 6 years of service, in exchange for a 4-year additional service-obligation commitment — a real service commitment, not paperwork-only. Must be requested via milConnect while still serving; cannot be initiated after separation. Dependent children must wait until the member has 10+ years of service to start using it.
veteran_benefits.va_home_loan_note: VA-backed home loans generally require no down payment and no monthly PMI for veterans with full entitlement, subject to lender underwriting (credit/income/DTI) and a funding fee (1.25%-3.3% of loan amount depending on down payment and first/subsequent use, waived for veterans drawing service-connected disability compensation).
veteran_benefits.va_healthcare_priority_group_note: VA assigns one of 8 priority groups at enrollment based on service-connected disability rating, combat/toxic-exposure service history, income, and other VA-benefit receipt — not by specialty. This member's group assignment depends entirely on their individual disability rating and income profile, not their rating/MOS/designator.
veteran_benefits.state_benefit_variance_note: State veteran benefits (property-tax exemptions, income-tax treatment, tuition waivers, etc.) are set by individual state legislatures and are NOT a single national figure (data/veteran-benefits.json). Verify against the veteran's specific state of legal residence.

education_benefits.gi_bill_summary: Post-9/11 GI Bill pays full tuition/fees at public schools (in-state rate applies to all users) or up to $29,920.95/yr (2025-2026 AY) at private/foreign schools, plus a housing allowance keyed to the school's zip code and a $1,000/yr book stipend — scaled by the eligibility tier above. (data/education-benefits.json)
